DDR4.description: The dominant RAM standard from 2014 through 2022, still widely used in 2026 for budget builds on older Intel 12th/13th/14th Gen and AMD AM4 platforms. Cheaper and more mature than DDR5, with lower theoretical bandwidth.

kits.name: Generic DDR4-3200 16GB (2x8GB)
kits.desc: The baseline standard for budget builds on AM4/LGA1700 platforms. 3200MHz at CL16 hits the sweet spot for AMD's Infinity Fabric on Ryzen 5000 series. Adequate for 1080p gaming and general productivity.

kits.name: Corsair Vengeance LPX DDR4-3600 32GB (2x16GB)
kits.desc: The most popular DDR4 capacity/speed combo for serious gaming and content creation builds. 32GB headroom comfortably handles modern games plus background apps, streaming software, and browser tabs simultaneously.
cons: Not expandable cheaply if DDR4 supply dwindles further

kits.name: G.Skill Trident Z RGB DDR4-3600 32GB (2x16GB)
kits.desc: Tighter CL16 timings than the budget-tier kits for better real-world latency-sensitive gaming performance, plus RGB for aesthetic builds. A favorite in showcase gaming PCs on the AM4/LGA1700 platforms.
cons: DDR4 ceiling approaching as DDR5 matures

DDR5.description: The current-generation RAM standard since 2022, mandatory on Intel Core Ultra (Arrow Lake) and AMD AM5 platforms. Higher bandwidth, EXPO/XMP 3.0 profiles, and on-die ECC, at a price premium that has shrunk significantly by 2026.

kits.name: Kingston Fury Beast DDR5-5200 16GB (2x8GB)
kits.desc: Entry point into DDR5 for budget AM5 or Arrow Lake builds. JEDEC-friendly 5200MHz speed runs without needing aggressive XMP/EXPO tuning, keeping stability high on cheaper motherboards.
cons: Higher CAS latency vs faster kits

kits.name: G.Skill Flare X5 DDR5-6000 32GB (2x16GB)
kits.desc: The well-known sweet spot for AMD Ryzen 7000/9000 series - 6000MHz CL30 is the optimal Infinity Fabric ratio for AM5, delivering the best real-world gaming latency without paying for unnecessary speed beyond this point.

kits.name: Corsair Dominator Titanium DDR5-6400 64GB (2x32GB)
kits.desc: High-capacity kit aimed at content creators, AI/ML hobbyists running local LLMs, and professionals juggling large Premiere/DaVinci/Blender projects alongside dozens of browser tabs and VMs.
cons: Overkill for pure gaming use cases

kits.name: Kingston Fury Renegade DDR5-7200 32GB (2x16GB)
kits.desc: Current-gen high-speed kit tuned for Intel Arrow Lake and AMD AM5 platforms that can take advantage of higher memory controller headroom. Notable gaming uplift in memory-bandwidth-sensitive titles.
pros: Strong gaming uplift in sensitive titles
cons: Needs a capable motherboard memory controller to hit rated speed reliably
